arabic_text:
قُلْ كَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ شَهِيدًا بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ بِعِبَادِهِ خَبِيرًا بَصِيرًا
قُلْ كَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ شَهِيدًا بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ ۖ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِى السَّمَوَتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۗ وَالَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا بِالْبَطِلِ وَكَفَرُوا بِاللَّهِ أُولَئِكَ هُمُ الْخَسِرُونَ
english_text:
(17:96) Allah is enough as a witness between me and you / Allah has complete knowledge of his slaves
(29:52) Allah is enough between me and you as a witness / He knows what is in the heavens and earth. And they who have believed in falsehood and disbelieved in Allah - it is those who are the losers.
alt_text: Side-by-side comparison of two Quran verses (17:96 and 29:52) with English annotations, showing how each phrases the same idea — Allah being a sufficient witness between the prophet and his audience — with different word-orders (shahidan baynī / baynī shahidan).
notes: Quran 17:96 and 29:52 compared — classic exemplar of Quranic word-order precision. Citation marks 17:96 primarily.
